_meta.description: Patterns generalised by learning-loop.js from real leak incidents. Loaded by banned-content-patterns.js at scan time and merged with the static registry. Append-only — never delete; if a pattern becomes too broad, narrow it via regex modification, don't remove.

learned.description: JSON-LD schema type names rendered as H1-H6 headings instead of being embedded in <script type='application/ld+json'>
learned.false_positive_rate: low — schema names rarely appear in legitimate body content
learned.promotion_to_registry: Promoted to banned-content-patterns.js evaluationLabels on 16 April 2026

learned.description: Bare 'WHY:' label without trailing 'SUCCESS' or 'SUCCEEDED' — shorter variant of WHY SUCCESS: that was missed by the original registry
learned.false_positive_rate: moderate — 'WHY:' could appear in legitimate FAQ content. Mitigation: only flag at start of line/paragraph.

learned.description: Bracketed audit checklist marker [x] followed by E-E-A-T text within the same line

candidates.description: Risk/priority classification language that may indicate internal evaluation leaking — needs more occurrences before promotion to learned
